mutex.c(3) Library Functions Manual mutex.c(3)

NAME

mutex.c - Per-thread mutex lock stack for deadlock detection.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct thread_lock_stack_t
struct thread_registry_entry_t
struct deadlock_state_t

Macros


#define MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH 64
#define MAX_THREADS 256
#define MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES 16
#define MAX_CYCLE_LEN 64
DFS-based cycle detection in the waits-for graph Returns cycle start index if found, -1 otherwise Fills cycle_path with indices of threads in the cycle (if found). #define COND_DEADLOCK_THRESHOLD_NS (5ULL * 1000000000ULL)

Functions


void mutex_stack_push_pending (uintptr_t mutex_key, const char *mutex_name)
void mutex_stack_mark_locked (uintptr_t mutex_key)
void mutex_stack_pop (uintptr_t mutex_key)
int mutex_stack_get_current (mutex_stack_entry_t *out_entries, int max_entries)
int mutex_stack_get_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t ***out_stacks, int **out_stack_counts, int *out_thread_count)
void mutex_stack_free_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t **stacks, int *stack_counts, int thread_count)
void mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks (void)
Detect circular wait deadlocks using DFS-based cycle detection. bool debug_sync_is_cleanup_in_progress (void)
void debug_sync_check_cond_deadlocks (void)
Check all condition variables for deadlocks. int mutex_stack_init (void)
void mutex_stack_cleanup_current_thread (void)
void mutex_stack_cleanup (void)

Detailed Description

Per-thread mutex lock stack for deadlock detection.

Date

February 2026

Tracks which mutexes are held and pending per thread. Detects circular wait patterns (classic deadlock condition).

Definition in file debug/mutex.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define COND_DEADLOCK_THRESHOLD_NS (5ULL * 1000000000ULL)

Function Documentation

void debug_sync_check_cond_deadlocks (void )

Check all condition variables for deadlocks. Scans all registered condition variables and logs warnings for any that have been waiting without signal for longer than COND_DEADLOCK_THRESHOLD_NS. Called periodically by the debug thread (every 100ms).

Skips checks during shutdown to avoid accessing freed memory.

Definition at line 688 of file debug/mutex.c.

688                                            {
689   if (debug_sync_is_cleanup_in_progress()) {
690     return;
691   }
692   named_registry_for_each(cond_deadlock_check_callback, NULL);
693 }




References debug_sync_is_cleanup_in_progress(), and
    named_registry_for_each().

void
  mutex_stack_cleanup (void )


Definition at line 735 of file debug/mutex.c.


735                                {
736   // Signal shutdown to prevent new allocations from threads still running
737   atomic_store_bool(&g_shutting_down, true);
738 
739   // Manually free all stacks in the registry
740   // This must happen BEFORE deleting the TLS key to avoid double-free
741   // (destructor won't run on still-running threads until they exit, by which time
742   // these stacks will already be freed)
743   int count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
744   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
745     if (g_thread_registry[i].stack) {
746       // Use raw free() - stacks are allocated with raw malloc(), not SAFE_CALLOC()
747       free(g_thread_registry[i].stack);
748       g_thread_registry[i].stack = NULL;
749     }
750   }
751 
752   // Now delete the TLS key - destructors will see NULL in registry and skip freeing
753   // For any threads that haven't been registered yet, destructor will free directly
754   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_tls_initialized)) {
755     ascii_tls_key_delete(g_tls_mutex_stack);
756     atomic_store_bool(&g_tls_initialized, false);
757   }
758 
759   // Clear registry on cleanup using atomic operations
760   atomic_store_int(&g_thread_registry_count, 0);
761 }






References ascii_tls_key_delete(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_load_int(),
    atomic_store_bool(), and atomic_store_int().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






void
  mutex_stack_cleanup_current_thread (void )


Definition at line 704 of file debug/mutex.c.


704                                               {
705   // Explicitly free the current thread's TLS stack
706   // This is used to prevent leaks when TLS destructors might not run reliably
707   // (e.g., debug threads exiting before mutex_stack_cleanup() deletes the TLS key)
708 
709   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_tls_initialized)) {
710     return; // TLS not initialized, nothing to clean up
711   }
712 
713   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = (thread_lock_stack_t *)ascii_tls_get(g_tls_mutex_stack);
714   if (!stack) {
715     return; // No stack allocated for this thread
716   }
717 
718   // Clear from TLS
719   ascii_tls_set(g_tls_mutex_stack, NULL);
720 
721   // Update registry if this thread is registered
722   thread_id_t current_thread = asciichat_thread_self();
723   int count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
724   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
725     if (asciichat_thread_equal(g_thread_registry[i].thread_id, current_thread) && g_thread_registry[i].stack == stack) {
726       g_thread_registry[i].stack = NULL; // Mark as freed in registry
727       break;
728     }
729   }
730 
731   // Free the stack (raw free to match raw malloc above)
732   free(stack);
733 }






References ascii_tls_get(), ascii_tls_set(),
    asciichat_thread_equal(), asciichat_thread_self(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_load_int(), and
  thread_id.


Referenced by debug_sync_final_cleanup().






void
  mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks (void )


Detect circular wait deadlocks using DFS-based cycle detection.
    Detects both same-thread and multi-thread deadlock patterns of any
  length:




Same-thread deadlock:



  
•

  
Thread tries to acquire a mutex it already holds (recursive lock on
      non-recursive mutex)




Multi-thread circular wait (2-way, 3-way, N-way):


    
  
  • Uses DFS to detect cycles in the "waits-for" graph
    • 
  
  • Reports all threads involved in the cycle
    • 



Definition at line 520 of file debug/mutex.c.


520                                         {
521   mutex_stack_entry_t **all_stacks = NULL;
522   int *stack_counts = NULL;
523   int thread_count = 0;
524 
525   if (mutex_stack_get_all_threads(&all_stacks, &stack_counts, &thread_count) != 0) {
526     return;
527   }
528 
529   // Check each thread for deadlock conditions
530   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
531     thread_lock_stack_t *stack_a = g_thread_registry[i].stack;
532     uintptr_t waiting_for = thread_waiting_for_mutex(stack_a);
533 
534     if (waiting_for == 0)
535       continue; // Thread not waiting
536 
537     // Same-thread deadlock: thread trying to acquire a mutex it already holds
538     if (thread_holds_mutex(stack_a, waiting_for)) {
539       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"));
540       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "║  ⚠️  DEADLOCK DETECTED: Same-thread Recursive Lock  ⚠️  ║"));
541       log_error("%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"));
542       log_error("  Thread Address:        0x%lx", (unsigned long)g_thread_registry[i].thread_id);
543       log_error("  Mutex:                 0x%lx", waiting_for);
544       log_error("  Issue:                 Thread attempts recursive lock on non-recursive mutex");
545       continue;
546     }
547 
548     // Multi-thread circular wait: use DFS to detect cycles
549     int cycle_path[MAX_CYCLE_LEN];
550     int cycle_len = 0;
551     int cycle_start = detect_cycle_dfs(thread_count, i, cycle_path, &cycle_len);
552 
553     if (cycle_start >= 0 && cycle_len > 1) {
554       // Collect mutexes involved in this deadlock
555       uintptr_t cycle_mutexes[MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES];
556       int mutex_count = 0;
557       for (int k = 0; k < cycle_len && mutex_count < MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES; k++) {
558         int thread_idx = cycle_path[k];
559         thread_lock_stack_t *stack = g_thread_registry[thread_idx].stack;
560         uintptr_t waiting_for = thread_waiting_for_mutex(stack);
561         if (waiting_for != 0) {
562           cycle_mutexes[mutex_count++] = waiting_for;
563         }
564       }
565 
566       // Check if mutexes are different from last deadlock
567       bool is_new_deadlock = deadlock_mutexes_changed(cycle_mutexes, mutex_count);
568       if (is_new_deadlock) {
569         update_deadlock_state(cycle_mutexes, mutex_count);
570       }
571 
572       // Cycle detected! Build complete message in one string
573       char cycle_msg[4096];
574       int msg_len = 0;
575 
576       // Leading newline and header box
577       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "\n%s\n",
578                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╔═════════════════════════════════╗"));
579       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "%s\n",
580                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "║  DEADLOCK: Circular Wait Cycle  ║"));
581       msg_len += snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "%s\n",
582                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "╚═════════════════════════════════╝"));
583 
584       // Print each thread in the cycle
585       for (int k = 0; k < cycle_len; k++) {
586         int thread_idx = cycle_path[k];
587         int next_thread_idx = cycle_path[(k + 1) % cycle_len];
588 
589         thread_lock_stack_t *current_stack = g_thread_registry[thread_idx].stack;
590         uintptr_t current_waiting = thread_waiting_for_mutex(current_stack);
591 
592         char thread_name[256], mutex_name[256], held_by_name[256];
593         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR(g_thread_registry[thread_idx].thread_id, thread_name, sizeof(thread_name));
594         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR(current_waiting, mutex_name, sizeof(mutex_name));
595         NAMED_GET_BY_PTR(g_thread_registry[next_thread_idx].thread_id, held_by_name, sizeof(held_by_name));
596 
597         msg_len +=
598             snprintf(cycle_msg + msg_len, sizeof(cycle_msg) - msg_len, "  T%d: %s waits for %s (held by %s)%s",
599                      k + 1, thread_name, mutex_name, held_by_name, k < cycle_len - 1 ? "\n" : "");
600       }
601 
602       // Log repeated deadlock detections (skip first call, throttle subsequent ones)
603       if (!is_new_deadlock) {
604         log_error_every(1000000, "%s", cycle_msg); // 1000000 µs = 1 second
605       }
606     }
607   }
608 
609   mutex_stack_free_all_threads(all_stacks, stack_counts, thread_count);
610 }






References colored_string(), MAX_CYCLE_LEN,
    MAX_CYCLE_MUTEXES, mutex_stack_free_all_threads(),
    mutex_stack_get_all_threads(), thread_lock_stack_t::stack, and
    thread_id.






void
  mutex_stack_free_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t ** stacks, int *
  stack_counts, int thread_count)


Definition at line 343 of file debug/mutex.c.


343                                                                                                      {
344 
345   if (!stacks || !stack_counts)
346     return;
347 
348   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
349     SAFE_FREE(stacks[i]);
350   }
351 
352   SAFE_FREE(stacks);
353   SAFE_FREE(stack_counts);
354 }






Referenced by mutex_stack_detect_deadlocks().






int
  mutex_stack_get_all_threads (mutex_stack_entry_t *** out_stacks, int **
  out_stack_counts, int * out_thread_count)


Definition at line 280 of file debug/mutex.c.


280                                                                                                                   {
281 
282   if (!out_stacks || !out_stack_counts || !out_thread_count) {
283     return -1;
284   }
285 
286   // Read the thread count with acquire semantics (lock-free, no mutex needed)
287   // Use memory_order_seq_cst to ensure we get a consistent snapshot
288   int thread_count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
289 
290   // Allocate arrays for threads in the registry
291   // Note: SAFE_MALLOC takes bytes as first parameter, not count
292   *out_stacks = SAFE_MALLOC(thread_count * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t *), mutex_stack_entry_t **);
293   *out_stack_counts = SAFE_MALLOC(thread_count * sizeof(int), int *);
294 
295   if (!*out_stacks || !*out_stack_counts) {
296     SAFE_FREE(*out_stacks);
297     SAFE_FREE(*out_stack_counts);
298     return -1;
299   }
300 
301   // Copy each thread's stack from the registry
302   // Note: the thread count can change concurrently, so we re-read it in the loop
303   // to avoid accessing out-of-bounds memory if threads exit during iteration
304   int actual_count = 0;
305   for (int i = 0; i < thread_count; i++) {
306     // Re-check thread count in case registry shrank
307     int current_registry_count = atomic_load_int(&g_thread_registry_count);
308     if (i >= current_registry_count) {
309       break;
310     }
311 
312     thread_lock_stack_t *src = g_thread_registry[i].stack;
313     if (!src) {
314       // Stack not yet initialized for this thread, skip it
315       (*out_stack_counts)[i] = 0;
316       (*out_stacks)[i] = NULL;
317       continue;
318     }
319 
320     // Defensively get depth - it could change or be freed by another thread
321     int depth = 0;
322     if (src && src->depth >= 0 && src->depth < MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH) {
323       depth = src->depth;
324     }
325 
326     (*out_stack_counts)[i] = depth;
327 
328     if (depth > 0) {
329       (*out_stacks)[i] = SAFE_MALLOC(depth * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t), mutex_stack_entry_t *);
330       if ((*out_stacks)[i]) {
331         memcpy((*out_stacks)[i], src->stack, depth * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t));
332         actual_count++;
333       }
334     } else {
335       (*out_stacks)[i] = NULL;
336     }
337   }
338 
339   *out_thread_count = actual_count;
340   return 0;
341 }






References atomic_load_int(),
    thread_lock_stack_t::depth, MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH, and
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack.

int
  mutex_stack_get_current (mutex_stack_entry_t * out_entries, int
  max_entries)


Definition at line 265 of file debug/mutex.c.


265                                                                                {
266   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
267   if (!stack || !out_entries) {
268     return 0;
269   }
270 
271   int count = (stack->depth < max_entries) ? stack->depth : max_entries;
272   memcpy(out_entries, stack->stack, count * sizeof(mutex_stack_entry_t));
273   return stack->depth; // Return actual depth even if truncated
274 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth, and
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack.

  mutex_stack_init (void )

void
  mutex_stack_mark_locked (uintptr_t mutex_key)


Definition at line 234 of file debug/mutex.c.


234                                                   {
235   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
236   if (!stack || stack->depth == 0) {
237     return;
238   }
239 
240   // Mark the top of the stack as locked
241   int top = stack->depth - 1;
242   if (stack->stack[top].mutex_key == mutex_key) {
243     stack->stack[top].state = MUTEX_STACK_STATE_LOCKED;
244     stack->stack[top].timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
245   }
246 
247   // Thread-local only. Registry is populated on-demand by mutex_stack_get_all_threads()
248 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth,
    thread_lock_stack_t::stack, and time_get_ns().






void
  mutex_stack_pop (uintptr_t mutex_key)


Definition at line 250 of file debug/mutex.c.


250                                           {
251   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
252   if (!stack || stack->depth == 0) {
253     return;
254   }
255 
256   // Validate top matches
257   int top = stack->depth - 1;
258   if (stack->stack[top].mutex_key == mutex_key) {
259     stack->depth--;
260   }
261 
262   // Thread-local only. Registry is populated on-demand by mutex_stack_get_all_threads()
263 }

void
  mutex_stack_push_pending (uintptr_t mutex_key, const char *
  mutex_name)


Definition at line 218 of file debug/mutex.c.


218                                                                            {
219   thread_lock_stack_t *stack = get_thread_local_stack();
220   if (!stack || stack->depth >= MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH) {
221     return;
222   }
223 
224   // Register this thread in the global registry on first mutex use
225   register_thread_if_needed();
226 
227   stack->stack[stack->depth].mutex_key = mutex_key;
228   stack->stack[stack->depth].mutex_name = mutex_name;
229   stack->stack[stack->depth].state = MUTEX_STACK_STATE_PENDING;
230   stack->stack[stack->depth].timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
231   stack->depth++;
232 }






References thread_lock_stack_t::depth,
    MUTEX_STACK_MAX_DEPTH, thread_lock_stack_t::stack, and
    time_get_ns().
